AOS ERP System

Welcome

Pick a module below to get started — every tab is one tap away.

Locating… --:-- • --°
Quick Access

Voucher details

This deducts the same amount from this site's Main Cash Box at the same time, since Sub is funded out of Main. This is an internal fund transfer, not revenue — it will never appear on the Income Statement.
Auto-suggested from the voucher type — feeds the Full Accounting System (General Ledger, Income Statement, Balance Sheet) automatically. Change it if this voucher belongs somewhere else.
Journal Vouchers are manual double-entry adjustments (e.g. recording a fixed asset, a payable, or an owner's capital injection) — they don't move cash, so pick exactly which account is debited and which is credited.
Posts to the site's LYD cash box. Switch to USD to enter and post this voucher's amount in US Dollars instead — it will post to the matching USD cash box.
For reference only — no longer part of the serial number.

Charge / expense lines * at least one line with description + amount

Voucher total 0.000

Amount in words (view only — auto-calculated from the lines above)

Preview

Shared log — visible to everyone on the team using this system.

Searches the shared voucher log across all months. Leave a field blank to ignore it.

Voucher

Edit voucher

Re-categorize this voucher in the Full Accounting System (General Ledger, Income Statement, Balance Sheet).

Charge / expense lines

Amount in words (view only — auto-calculated, cannot be edited)

Statement period

Pick any date range you like — a day, a week, a custom period, a whole year — and generate it whenever you need it.
Pick a period above and click "Generate statement".

Cash Statement — physical count reconciliation

Pick a site and month. The book totals (Total Cash, Total Booked Expenses, Balance per Books) and the Open Vouchers total are pulled automatically from the Treasury ledger — only the physical bill/coin count and any prepaid/foreign-currency notes on hand need to be entered by hand, exactly like the paper form. The Overage/Shortage at the bottom is then calculated for you.
Pick a site and month above, load, enter the physical count, then click "Generate Cash Statement".

Treasury balances — live, as of today

Daily balances report — all sites, all cash boxes

Shows every cash box's opening, cash in, cash out and closing balance for each day in the range — LYD and USD sit side by side in the same table, no separate table per currency. Built automatically from the movements you add below, no re-typing balances.
Pick a date range above and click "Generate daily report" to see every cash box's balance, day by day.

Voucher exchange rates (separate from the box rate below)

🔒 Read-only — unlock editing (admin) in the Treasury cash boxes card below to change these. These rates are only used to convert a USD voucher's total into its LYD equivalent for printing and for the Daily Movement Expenses report. Completely separate from the "Exchange rate" field below.

Buy rate — Payment vouchers

Used for Cash/Bank Payment, Petty Cash, Advance, Settlement, Journal, Refund, Pay Slip and Open Vouchers — any voucher where cash goes OUT.

Sell rate — Receipt vouchers

Used for Cash Receipts and Bank Receipt vouchers — any voucher where cash comes IN.

Treasury cash boxes — Main & Sub (LYD + USD) by site

🔒 Read-only — editing and saving cash box data requires an admin login. Viewing, generating statements, printing and exporting stay available below.
Each site is optional — fill in whichever site(s) you have data for and leave the rest blank. Viewing, generating the treasury statement, printing and exporting stay available to everyone — editing and saving cash box data is admin-only, unlocked with the admin password.

Treasury cash flow statement (printable)

Fill in whichever site(s) you have data for above, then click "Generate treasury statement".

Daily Movement Expenses

Every voucher you issue is posted here automatically, the same day, into the correct cash box — no re-typing. Business rule: at a site with a Sub Cash Box, every voucher is paid out of (or received into) the Sub Cash Box only; the Main Cash Box is never touched by an ordinary voucher — it only moves when you record an actual fund transfer yourself in the Treasury tab. A site with only a Main Cash Box uses that instead.
Or pick a period to see every movement recorded across a range of days (same site as above):

Open Vouchers & Pending Deposits

Open Vouchers (advances not yet settled)

Auto-listed from every "Open Vouchers - Main/Sub Cash Box" voucher issued for this site. Mark one Settled once its expense has been reconciled — this only changes its status on reports, it never touches any cash box balance (the cash already moved when the voucher was issued).

Open vouchers (manual entry, FYI)

For an open voucher that wasn't raised through New Voucher — e.g. one issued outside the system. Informational only, shows on this report only, and does not affect any cash box balance.

Pending deposits (FYI)

Informational only — e.g. salaries or receipts awaiting bank confirmation. Does not affect any cash box balance.
Pick a site and date above, then click "Show this day".

Passport database — current log (checked IN)

This log shows passports currently checked IN. Checking a passport OUT removes it from this log automatically; checking it back IN adds it back automatically. Use the checkbox to also see passports currently OUT.
#NamePassport No.NationalityJob No. (ID)Expiry DateStatusDate InDate OutNoteActions

Add passport

Passport movement log (daily record)

Use the "Check IN" / "Check OUT" buttons on the passport database above to record custody movements. Checking a passport IN or OUT is logged here automatically with today's date and time — every day's movements stay on record.

Manual Desert Pass Records — current log (checked IN)

A simple typed-in record book for desert passes issued outside the generator below (e.g. historical records, or passes issued by another office). This does not generate or print a pass — use "New Desert Pass" below for that. This log shows holders currently checked IN; checking a holder OUT removes them from this log automatically, and checking them back IN adds them back automatically.
#NameDesert Pass No.Passport No.NationalityProfessionDestinationValid fromValid toStatusDate InDate OutNoteActions

Add record

Desert Pass movement log (daily record)

Use the "Check IN" / "Check OUT" buttons on the records above to record when a desert pass holder leaves for site (OUT) or returns (IN). Every movement is logged automatically with today's date and time.
DateTimeNamePassport No.MovementNote

New Desert Pass

"Desert Pass" is a travel/site-access permit for the selected person. Generate it below, then print, save as PDF, or add a secure QR.

Batch-print Desert Passes (each person on a separate sheet)

Tick everyone who needs a pass for the same trip/period, fill the shared details, then generate one PDF with each person's pass on its own page.

Residence database

Operation Management

Keep one master list of field employees (one-time setup, or import from Excel), then each day just mark who is on leave or absent/sick — everyone else is assumed Active. The daily manpower report, grouped by field/site with totals, is generated automatically in the AOS format. Use the Site / Field filter in each tab to view, export, or issue the Employees Master and Daily Report for one site at a time, or leave it on "All Sites" for the combined company-wide report.

Employees master list

#AOS IDEmployee NameNationalityProfessionSite / FieldCategoryActions

Add employee

Manage sites / fields

Employees are always assigned from this list, so site names stay consistent everywhere. Adding, renaming, or removing a site requires the admin password.
#Site / Field nameEmployeesActions

Manage categories

These are the categories available for employees. The four default categories (Client Site, Camp Support, Staff/Office, Visitor) drive the report's grouping order and can't be removed. Adding or removing a custom category requires the admin password.
#Category nameTypeEmployeesActions

Daily attendance

#AOS IDEmployee NameNationalityProfessionSite / FieldStatusReason (if not active)

Report preview

Manpower period report

Pick any period — a single day, a week, a month, a quarter, a year, or a custom date range — and get a combined attendance report for that whole period: total days Active / On Leave / Absent per employee, and the same field/site summary as the Daily Report, totalled across every day in the range.

Period report preview

Site Daily Movement

Track exactly who arrives and who leaves at each site, every day. Log movements in seconds under Daily Entry (paste a whole list of names at once), review every record ever logged in Movement Log, and pull a day-by-day breakdown for any month in Monthly Report. The Overview below always shows live totals and counts for whichever period and site you pick.

Add a site to see the breakdown.

Daily entry — arrivals & departures

Pick the site and date, then paste or type one name per line into "Arrived" and/or "Left" and click the matching button. Each name becomes its own row in the log, stamped with the current time.
Already logged someone without a note, or need to fix one? Click ✎ Note next to any row below — notes can be added or edited for anyone, any time.

Opening roster — first day of a new month

If these people are already on site (carried over from before), paste their names here instead of using "Arrived" above — they're counted toward the headcount immediately, but won't show up as new Arrivals in reports. No need to mark anyone Left either; this only adds people, it never removes them.

Currently on site — —

Names present from previous days carry forward automatically — nobody needs to be re-typed as "Arrived" every day. Tick anyone who has left as of this date and click "Mark ticked as Left", or type brand-new arrivals in the box above.

Manage sites

Sites listed here are the ones offered everywhere in this module (Daily Entry, Movement Log, Monthly Report). Removing a site here does not delete any movement records already logged against it.
Pick a site from the list above — it's the same site list used in the Food Cost tab, so headcount here will automatically link to that site's Food Cost entries. Need a site that isn't in Food Cost? Type a custom name instead.
#Site nameMovement recordsActions

Opening Count (first month only)

If a site already had people on it before you started logging daily arrivals/departures here, set a one-time starting headcount below — for your very first month of using this module. It's meant to be set once and then left alone; every arrival/departure logged after that adjusts the running total from this starting point. Requires the admin password.
SiteOpening CountAs of dateActions
Adding, renaming, or deleting a site above now requires the admin password.
Clears every logged arrival/departure/movement record for every site. The site list itself is kept. Requires the admin password.

Report

Generates a branded, print-ready report of the current status — same information as above, formatted like your other official statements. Pick what to include below, any time you need it.

Custom Lists

Simple tracking lists anyone can define here — a name plus a few fields — without touching any code. Once a list is created it appears below for every signed-in staff member immediately, with a plain add / edit / delete screen. Good for things like "a new Passports-style list" that don't need a custom calculation, PDF layout, or workflow of their own.

New list

Fields — each row becomes one column in the list. Choose a type per field.

List

Add row

Word Sheet

A blank document you can type into directly, or upload an existing Word (.docx) or text file to continue editing — then download it back as a .docx, export a PDF, or print.

Excel Sheet

A blank spreadsheet grid — click a cell to select it, then type to overwrite it or press Enter/F2 (or double-click) to edit it in place; click-drag or Shift+Arrow to select a range. Upload an existing Excel (.xlsx) or CSV file, then download it back as .xlsx, export a PDF, or print. Formulas start with "=" and support + − × ÷, parentheses, cell references (A1), ranges (A1:A5), and SUM / AVERAGE / MIN / MAX / COUNT — e.g. =SUM(A1:A5) or =A1*B1.
A1

📧 Outlook

Compose an email here, then Send opens it in your computer's own email app (Outlook, Mail, Gmail, etc.) with everything already filled in — this app doesn't send email itself. Save a message as a Draft to finish later, or add someone to Quick Contacts so their address is one click away next time.

New message

Quick Contacts

A short personal address book — click a contact to fill "To" above.
NameEmailActions

Drafts

ToSubjectSavedActions

Library

Organize your Word, Excel, PowerPoint and PDF files into named sections. Add a brand-new Word/Excel file, or import an existing .docx/.txt/.xlsx/.xls/.csv/.pdf/.pptx file into any section. Open a Word or Excel file in this app to type, format, print or export it using the same tools as Word Sheet / Excel Sheet — a PDF opens as a read-only viewer right here instead, since PDFs aren't editable. PowerPoint files aren't editable in this app either — use Open on PC to open them in PowerPoint. Choose Open on PC on any file to download it so it opens in Word, Excel, PowerPoint or your PDF reader on your computer. A section can also be password-protected by an admin — see the 🔒 button on any section.

📕 PDF

Loading…

Payroll

Set each employee's basic salary, allowances and deductions once under Employees, then each month use Monthly Run to generate everyone's pay and review/adjust it. Finalize locks those numbers in for the month — it does not touch your books. When you're ready, Post to Ledger issues a real "Pay Slip" voucher per finalized employee (posting into the Vouchers Log, Treasury, General Ledger, Income Statement and Balance Sheet, exactly like any other voucher) and pays out of this office's cash box. Overview below only counts posted payroll; History keeps every month regardless of status.
Allowance & Deduction Types are managed separately and are never touched by this button.

Pick a period below and it automatically works out trips, hours and cost per day for the range — for one person or everyone. Group by Person to see who the overtime is credited to, or by Driver to see who actually made the trips (trip-based entries only).

Pick a period above.

Default day-of-week overtime rates

Set an hourly rate, a per-trip price, and/or a fixed daily amount for each day of the week — whichever kind applies is used to auto-suggest the rate when a new entry is logged for that day, based on the entry's chosen type (Hours-based, Trip-based, or Fixed amount). Always editable per entry. A person can override these with their own rates below, under "Overtime people". Hidden by default — admin password required to view or change these rates.
DayHourly OT ratePrice per tripFixed daily amount

Overtime people

Turn overtime tracking on for whichever people need it — employees, drivers, transport, or visitors — and pick their default pay type — by extra hours, by extra trips (drivers), or a fixed amount. This is just the default suggestion; you can pick a different type for any individual entry below.
PersonSiteCurrencyOvertime onModeCustom ratesRecord

Custom day rates —

DayHourly OT ratePrice per tripFixed daily amount

Step 1 — Site

Pick the site once here before logging any overtime below — every entry you save is automatically recorded under it, so there's no need to re-pick a site for each entry. Change it any time you need to switch sites — that's open to everyone. Adding a brand-new site to the list requires the Admin password. This list is shared with the Food Cost tab — a site added here (or there) becomes available in both.

Log overtime

Logging to site: — none selected — (change it in Step 1 above). For Trip-based entries, the "Person" is only whoever was transported — the cost is always calculated for and credited to the Driver, never to that person.
Trip details below (driver, duty start, journey time, from/to) only apply to — and are only required for — Trip-based entries. Hours-based and Fixed-amount overtime works for any person and skips these. Each saved entry counts as exactly 1 trip — if the same person made more than one trip that day, save a separate entry for each trip (with its own times/route). Multiple trips the same day are automatically shown as one combined row in the log below. Totals for any day, week, month or custom range are added up automatically in the reports below — nothing to count by hand.

Overtime log — selected month

Filter the log below by month, site or person.
DateDaySiteDriverPersonDuty StartJourney TimeFromToTypeTripsHoursRate/PriceCostNoteActions

Downloadable Report

Generates a formal, letterhead report — voucher activity, live treasury balances, and a full balance-by-site table for your selected period & site — ready to print or save as PDF.
Click "Generate report" above once the dashboard has finished loading.

Full Accounting System

Chart of Accounts, General Ledger, Trial Balance, Income Statement and Balance Sheet — built automatically from every voucher already being issued. Nothing extra to enter except picking an Account on each voucher (auto-suggested). Everything here updates the moment a voucher is issued, edited, or deleted, and every account is synced to your shared Google Sheet.

Chart of Accounts

Every voucher posts to one of these accounts. Admin only — click "Unlock" to add, rename, or retire an account. Changes here affect every report below immediately.
CodeNameTypeActive

Food Cost, by site

Sets the shared product list, prices and sites used by both this tool and the Food Stock tool (now under the Stock tab in the nav bar) — Food Cost totals what each site used, while Food Stock keeps a running inventory of what each site has on hand. Everything is saved on this device and synced to your shared Google Sheet automatically in the background — use "Export to Excel" / "Download PDF" any time you need a file.

Totals from entries saved to the log (Step 3) for each site, for the period selected below.

Upload a receipt below to see purchases here.

Step 1 — Product prices (all sites)

Scan a food-purchase receipt with your camera, or import a photo/PDF — a PDF with several receipts (one per page) is read page by page and each page is treated as its own receipt. Items, quantities and prices are read automatically (in Arabic), then you review and confirm before anything is added to the price list and Stock. Items move to the Kitchen automatically later, whenever they're taken out of Stock (Quantity Out in Step 3's "Add Movement").

Step 2 — Sites

If a site was ever renamed before this fix existed, its old name can be left behind in past records — which makes site-filtered totals (like Kitchen Items' or Food Stock's "Out — Total Food Consumption") show 0 or too low for that history, even though Food Cost's own Grand Total (which isn't filtered by site) still includes it. Click this to find any such leftover name and relink it to the correct current site.
No sites yet — click "+ Add site" to create the first one.

Quantities used

Site total: 0.000 LYD

Step 3 — Save today's entry to the log

Food Cost normally tracks one running total per site. To build daily / weekly / monthly / quarterly / annual reports, save a dated snapshot here whenever you finish entering a period's quantities — it records each site's current quantities and cost under that date. You can then generate a report for any range below. Saving also automatically records the same quantities as consumed in Kitchen Items ("Out"), dated this same day — nothing to record twice. Food Stock's on-hand quantities are left untouched by this.
DateSiteTotal (LYD)Actions

Step 4 — All sites together

Both buttons require the admin password. "Reset recorded data" clears entered quantities and saved log entries only — product prices and the site list are kept. "Reset EVERYTHING" also wipes prices, sites and site links.

Step 5 — Food Cost Statement (PDF)

Generates a formal statement — same layout as your vouchers and Cash Flow Statement — showing every site's cost and a grand total, ready to print or save as PDF.
Click "Generate statement" above once you've entered site quantities.

Step 6 — Period reports (daily / weekly / monthly / quarterly / annually / custom)

Builds a cost report from the saved log entries above, for any period you need.

Period report preview

Step 7 — Food Cost per Person (linked with Site Daily Movement)

Joins each logged entry above with the headcount recorded for its site in Site Daily Movement, so you can see the actual food expense and the cost per person for any day, week, month, quarter, year, or custom period — for one site or all of them. Link a site to its Site Daily Movement counterpart in Step 2 first (or leave it — an exact name match links automatically). Sites without a link still show their food cost total here; headcount and cost/person just show as "—" until linked.
"Deduct persons" is subtracted from that day's recorded Site Daily Movement headcount before working out cost per person — e.g. for people counted there who don't actually eat at camp. Applied to every day in the report.

Food Cost per Person — preview

Step 8 — Purchase Receipts Report (any period)

Every receipt uploaded in Step 1 ("📷 Scan receipt"), combined — with quantity, unit price and amount for every item — for any date range you need. Shows every line as scanned, plus an item-by-item summary totalled across all receipts in the period.

All receipt lines

By item — combined totals for the period

Period total: 0.000 LYD across 0 receipt(s)
Pick a period above and click "Generate report".

Food Stock, by site

Tracks how much of each food item is physically on hand at every site — separate from Food Cost's "quantity used". Prices are shared with the Food Cost tab (set them there, in Step 1). Sites are also shared — add or rename them from the Food Cost tab.

Opening / items in / items out / closing (LYD value), by site

SiteOpening (LYD)Items In (LYD)Opening + Items In (LYD)Items Out (LYD)Closing (LYD)
Add a site (Food Cost tab) to see the breakdown.
Opening Stock is read only from entries recorded here as "Opening Balance" — if you never record one, Opening shows 0. This logs every item's current on-hand quantity, at every site, as a dated "Opening Balance" entry. Items In and Items Out are then taken from the movement log alone, the third card is simply Opening + Items In, and Closing = Opening + Items In − Items Out. Safe to run any time; items that already have an Opening Balance entry are skipped automatically.

Step 1 — Site

No sites yet — go to the Food Cost tab and click "+ Add site" first.

Stock on hand

Don't see the item you need? Add it straight to the shared Food Cost price list from here — it will immediately appear in this list (and every other site's) too.
Once set up, day-to-day changes should go through Step 3 — Stock movement (in / out) below, not by editing quantities here directly — this table stays locked so it doesn't get bumped by accident. Unlock it only for an initial setup or an occasional correction.
📁 The stock list is hidden. Click "Show / hide stock list" above to open it.
Stock value: 0.000 LYD

Step 2 — All sites together

Step 3 — Stock movement (in / out)

Log deliveries in and consumption/waste out — the item's quantity on hand updates automatically. Made a mistake? Every row in the log below can be corrected with ✎ — date, item, quantity in/out, unit price and notes are all editable, and the quantity on hand (plus any linked Food Issuance and Kitchen Items record) is adjusted for you.
0.000
—
Total In (LYD): 0.000   Total Out (LYD): 0.000

Step 4 — Food Stock movement statement (any period)

Pick any date range — a day, a week, a month, a year — and generate an Opening Stock / Items In / Items Out / Closing Stock statement in Libyan Dinar, built from the movement log, in the same design as the Cash Flow Statement. Transactions are listed one line per item per day, with quantity in and quantity out side by side.

Step 5 — Food Stock Statement (PDF)

Same layout as your vouchers and Cash Flow Statement — a summary plus every item's quantity, price and value, for one site or all sites together, ready to print or save as PDF. Tick "Low stock items only" to build a statement that lists just the items at or below their reorder level.
Pick a site and period above, then click "Generate statement".
Click "Generate statement" above once you've entered stock on hand.

Overview

Opening / In / Out / Net / Closing for stock sitting in the Kitchen — same idea as the Food Stock Overview above. "In" is read from Food Stock's own movement log (its Quantity Out rows, at the price recorded on each date), so it always equals Food Stock's "Items Out (LYD)" for the same site and period. "Out" is linked straight to Food Cost's logged quantities used (Step 3 there).

Opening / in / out / closing (LYD value), by site

SiteOpening (LYD)In (LYD)Opening + Items In (LYD)Out (LYD)Closing (LYD)
Add a site (Food Cost tab) to see the breakdown.
"Out" is pulled from Food Cost's own logged quantities used (Step 3, "Save entry to log") for the same site and period — nothing to enter twice. Figures are clamped so nothing ever shows as negative, even if consumption logged for a period exceeds what came in.

Kitchen Items

Rows land here automatically from two places: every Quantity Out confirmed in Food Stock's "Add Movement" (Step 3) — already linked to that same site, "✎ Edit" also updates the underlying stock movement and Food Issuance record, and "🗑 Remove" reverses that stock-out movement and adds the quantity back to the site's on-hand stock; and every quantity logged as used in Food Cost's "Save entry to log" (Step 3 there) — these show as consumption "Out" rows, are not linked to any Food Stock movement (Food Stock is left untouched), and "✎ Edit" / "🗑 Remove" only change the Kitchen Items row itself, not the Food Cost log entry it came from. Already have stock sitting in the kitchen that was never entered as a movement? Use "➕ Add Opening Balance" above instead.
Kitchen value: 0.000 LYD   (0 items)

Food Issuance — Stock → Kitchen

Every time a Quantity Out is confirmed in Food Stock's "Add Movement" (Step 3), it's logged here automatically as issued to that same site's Kitchen — nothing to link or add by hand. Filter to any period below (daily, weekly, monthly, or a custom range) to see exactly what left Stock for the Kitchen, then export or print it.
0 line(s)   across 0 site(s)   •   0 distinct item(s)

Stock System

Add a site (warehouse, branch, store…) for this stock. Each site gets its own sheet of items (with colour/size, quantity, unit price and reorder level) plus its own stock-movement log. Everything is saved on this device and synced to your shared Google Sheet automatically in the background — use "Export to Excel" any time you need a workbook with an Items sheet and a Movement sheet per site, plus a summary.

Add a site to see the breakdown.
One-time setup: logs every item's current on-hand quantity, at every site in this category, as a dated "Opening Balance" movement — so Opening / Items In / Items Out / Net / Closing become real, trustworthy figures from today onward. Safe to run any time; items that already have an Opening Balance entry are skipped automatically.

Step 1 — Stock sites

No sites yet — click "+ Add site" to create the first one (e.g. Main Warehouse, Tripoli Branch).
Import reads a workbook with columns like Item / Colour / Size / Quantity (any order, header row anywhere on any sheet) and adds them as items under the site you choose — matching cells left blank (merged cells) are carried down from the row above, just like the source sheet.

Items

Once set up, day-to-day changes should go through Step 2 — Stock movement (in / out transactions) below, not by editing quantities here directly — this table stays locked so it doesn't get bumped by accident. Unlock it only for an initial setup or an occasional correction.
Stock value: 0.000 LYD

Set Reorder Level — by category

Pick a category (item name) and set its reorder level once — it's applied to every colour/size row under that name at this site, instead of typing it in one row at a time.

Subtotal by item — same item, all colours/sizes combined

Groups every colour/size of the same item name into one subtotal line — e.g. all "T-Shirt" colours added together — for this site only.

Step 2 — Stock movement (in / out transactions)

Log every time stock comes in or goes out for this type. Pick an item to have its quantity update automatically, or leave it as "Not linked to an item" to just keep a note — the same way the original movement sheet worked.
Total In (LYD): 0.000   Total Out (LYD): 0.000

Step 3 — All stock sites together

Step 4 — Stock movement statement (any period)

Pick any date range — a day, a week, a month, a year — and generate a statement in the same design as the Cash Flow Statement, built from the movement log above.
Pick a stock site and period above, then click "Generate statement".

Step 5 — Full Stock Items Report

See every item currently in stock, any time you need it — for one site or all sites combined — as a formal, letterhead report with quantity, price, stock value and low-stock flags. (To add items in bulk, use "Import items from Excel" in Step 1 above.)
Leave "As of date" blank for the live snapshot of stock as it stands right now. Pick a past date to reconstruct quantities as of that date from the stock-movement log — this is only fully accurate for movements that were linked to an item (Step 3), so treat it as an estimate if free-text movements were used too.
Pick a stock site above (or leave "All stock sites"), then click "Generate report".

AOS CalculatorQuick Finance Tool
 
0
Keyboard-ready: numbers, + − * /, Enter/= to calculate, Backspace to delete, Space to clear, % for percent, Ctrl+C to copy, Ctrl+V to paste.

History

Your recent calculations will appear here.
Exchange Rate ConverterLive rates · manual override supported
No rate loaded yet
—
 
Live rates are fetched from a public exchange-rate service and are indicative only — always confirm against your bank or treasury rate before issuing a voucher. If the device has no internet access, type a rate manually in the field above.

Translator

Type or paste text and it translates automatically as you type — no need to click anything. Works right in the browser — nothing is saved or shared.
Arabic English
0 characters
Powered by a free public translation service — best for a paragraph or two at a time. Translates automatically a moment after you stop typing; click "Translate now" for an instant re-check.

Serial number format

AOS-TRP-DDMMYY-00001

AOS — fixed company prefix  ·  TRP — 3-4 letter site code, one per site (see Settings > "Manage sites")  ·  DDMMYY — the voucher's date  ·  00001 — a 5-digit running sequence number, scoped per site per calendar month (each site starts counting from 1 on its own).

Sequence range for a site & month

Configured site/month ranges

Reset serial numbers

Resets the running count back to its starting number (whatever Start you've configured above — this doesn't erase that configuration, just restarts the count). Requires the Admin password. Clearing the Voucher Log or the Desert Pass Log already does this automatically for that log's numbers — these buttons are for resetting on demand, without clearing any data.

Connection diagnostics

If saving vouchers fails, use this to see the real reason instead of a generic message.

Staff accounts (admin only)

Add, remove, or change the name/password each staff member uses to sign in. Protected by a separate admin password.

Login history (admin only)

Every sign-in — who, account type, site, and the exact date & time — is recorded automatically the moment someone signs in, and synced to its own tab on the shared Google Sheet, the same way vouchers are. Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above.

Pick a date range above (staff member is optional) and click "Generate report" — defaults to today, so it's ready for a same-day report with one click.

🕒 Login Duration Report (admin only)

A dedicated, automatically-calculated report of how long each person actually worked, per day — built from their first sign-in and last sign-out time that day. Shows every user, every date in the range you pick, with total hours worked. Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above.

Pick a date range above (staff member is optional) and click "Generate report" — defaults to today, so it's ready for a same-day report with one click.

Manage sites (admin only)

Add a new site, relabel an existing one, toggle which cash boxes it has, or deactivate one you no longer use — every device picks this up automatically, no new file or redeploy needed. Sites are never deleted here: deactivating only hides a site from new-voucher/new-staff dropdowns, all of its historical vouchers and reports stay exactly as they are. A site's 3-4 letter code locks automatically once any voucher has been issued under it (changing it would break already-printed serial numbers) — deactivate it and add a new site instead if you need a different code. Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above.

Food Cost sites (admin only)

Add, rename, or delete the sites/camps used in the Food Cost tab. This is a separate list from "Manage sites" above — Food Cost keeps its own site list, the same way Food Stock and Clothes Stock do. Deleting a site here also deletes all of its saved quantities for that site (its logged history stays as it was). Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above.

App settings (admin only)

Change passwords, cash-count denominations, company branding, a couple of thresholds, and which tabs are shown app-wide. Saved here, these apply automatically on every device the next time it loads or refreshes the app — no new file to send around. Protected by the same admin password as Staff accounts above. (Brand-new *kinds* of features with their own unique logic are still code, not data — see Code.gs for how those now reach every device too, via a single hosted link instead of a resent file.)

Company branding

Shown on the lock screen, the app header, and every printed voucher.

Thresholds

Tabs shown in the app

Untick a tab to hide it for EVERYONE, app-wide — on top of (not instead of) each staff member's own checklist in "Manage staff accounts" above. Useful for quietly retiring a module nobody uses anymore, without touching any code. "Settings" can't be hidden this way, so nobody gets locked out.

🎭 Preview / demo mode

For showing the app to someone (e.g. a CEO demo) without exposing your real records. Turning this on safely tucks away everything currently in Food Cost and Site Daily Movement and replaces it with sample demo sites, prices, quantities and staff names so the app looks alive during a walkthrough. Your real data is not deleted — it's kept safely aside and comes back exactly as it was the moment you exit preview mode. Requires the admin password to turn on.

Note: this currently covers Food Cost and Site Daily Movement — the two modules used most for demos. Other tabs are unaffected either way.

🎭 Preview mode is currently ON — the app is showing sample demo data, not your real records.

⚠ Danger zone — reset all app data

This wipes every module's data on this device/browser — Food Cost, Food Stock, Stock Items, Site Daily Movement, Treasury, Operations Management, Vouchers, Custom Lists, Payroll, Overtime, Passports, Desert Pass, Residence, everything — including basics like sites, categories and prices. Company branding, passwords and sequence ranges are kept. This cannot be undone, and requires the admin password.

If this app is connected to a shared Google Sheet backend, this button only clears the local copy on this device — the shared sheet itself is not touched, so synced modules may pull the old data back down the next time they sync. Clear the relevant sheet tabs directly if you need the shared backend wiped too.

⚠ Danger zone — reset ALL recorded data (shared, keeps basic setup)

Unlike the button above (which only clears this device's local cache), this one reaches the shared Google Sheet and clears every module's recorded/transactional history at once — vouchers, treasury movements, Food Cost quantities/log/receipts/kitchen items/issuance, food & clothes requests, Food Stock & Stock Site quantities and movement logs, passports, desert passes (both logs), residence records, operation management employees, payroll employees & monthly runs, overtime entries, HR leave requests, Site Daily Movement logs, accommodation occupants, every custom list's rows, Outlook drafts, and login history.

Kept untouched (basic setup): Sites, Staff accounts, Chart of Accounts, App Settings, serial number ranges, Food Cost prices/site list/custom items/site links, Food Stock reorder levels, Stock Site's item catalog (names/colours/sizes/prices/reorder levels — only quantities are zeroed), Operation Management's site & category lists, Payroll's Allowance & Deduction Types, Overtime settings & per-employee rates, Site Daily Movement's site list, Accommodation's room/section layout (only occupant names are cleared), every custom list's column definitions, Outlook contacts, and the Library (not touched at all).

Requires the admin password, plus typing a confirmation phrase. This cannot be undone.

Edit Voucher

Password required

Add from Stock

Pick an item already tracked in the Stock tab to copy it into the Food Cost price list — its name, unit and price are carried over, and you can adjust anything before saving. Items already in the price list are marked "already added" but can still be picked again (e.g. to update the price).

Add New Product

Adds a product to the shared price list — it will also appear in Food Stock and every site's quantity list. Price is optional here; you can always set or change it later in the price list.
Set once here — this low-stock threshold applies to this product at every site in Food Stock, so it doesn't need to be re-entered site by site.

Edit Kitchen Item

This item came from a Food Stock Quantity Out movement — its quantity/price are kept in sync with that movement and its Food Issuance record automatically. To change its site or Food Cost product, remove it here and re-record the Quantity Out at the correct site/item instead.
This row was recorded automatically from a Food Cost log entry ("Save entry to log", Step 3) — it isn't linked to any Food Stock movement. Editing the quantity/price here only changes this Kitchen Items row; it won't change the Food Cost log entry or the "Out — Total Food Consumption" KPI, which always reads straight from the Food Cost log. To correct the actual consumption figure, edit or re-save the entry in Food Cost instead.

Edit stock movement

Change any detail of this movement — date, item, quantity in/out, unit price or notes. The item's quantity on hand is corrected automatically (the old figures are reversed and the new ones applied). If this movement has a Quantity Out, its matching Food Issuance record and Kitchen Items entry are updated to match; setting Quantity Out to zero removes them, and adding one creates them.
0.000

Set reorder levels by unit type

Every product on the price list is grouped below by its unit of measure. Enter one reorder level per unit type and it is applied to every product measured in that unit, at every site, in a single step — instead of typing the same figure into hundreds of rows. Leave a unit blank to leave those products exactly as they are.

Import price list from Excel

Choose how this file should be brought in before picking it.

Add Opening Balance — Kitchen Item

For stock already sitting in the kitchen that was never entered as a Food Stock movement (e.g. first-time setup). This creates an "Opening Balance" row directly in Kitchen Items for the item/site below — it does not add anything to Food Stock's movement history (Step 3) or change the site's on-hand stock, since this quantity is understood to already be with the kitchen, not the warehouse.

Review scanned receipt(s)

Reading the receipt…
Arabic name (as on receipt) English name (optional) Category Unit Qty Unit Price Amount Goes to
Each receipt/page gets its own date, vendor and invoice # (shown as a header row above its items) — uncheck any line the scan got wrong, fix the text/qty/prices, pick a category for new items (existing categories are suggested — type a new one to create it), and choose which Stock site it goes into. Nothing is saved until you confirm below. Items move to the Kitchen automatically later, whenever they're taken out of that site's Stock (Quantity Out in Add Movement).

Admin Access

Staff account management

🔒 Password required
